description: Migration up creates `app_search_index` table with exactly the columns and types listed in PRD §9.1
Primary key is `(tenant, object_type, object_id)` and `tenant` is NOT NULL
Migration is idempotent on `pg_trgm`: re-running CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS does not fail
Under Citus, `pg_dist_partition` shows `app_search_index` distributed by `tenant`
Without Citus extension, migration completes without invoking `create_distributed_table`
GIN index on `search_vector` is present and is selected by EXPLAIN for an `@@` query
GIN trgm index on `title` is selected by EXPLAIN for `title %% 'abc'`
GIN trgm index on `subtitle` is selected by EXPLAIN for `subtitle %% 'abc'`
btree indexes on `(tenant, source_updated_at DESC)` and `(tenant, object_type)` exist
Migration down drops the table; re-running up succeeds afterwards
All 27 entity types compile-time exhaustive-check via a switch over `SearchObjectType`
SearchDoc / AclMetadata interfaces require ACL fields when the entity has non-trivial ACL (compile-time)
`flattenBlockNote` extracts visible text from a real document's BlockNote JSON fixture
`flattenBlockNote` drops image data URI payloads (no `data:image/...` substring in output)
`flattenBlockNote` handles deeply nested lists and inline marks without throwing
`flattenMarkdown` strips headings, lists, link syntax, bold/italic markers, code fences, leaves plain text
`flattenJsonbPayload` concatenates string leaves and skips keys matching the secret regex (`password|secret|token|api_key|authorization`)
`flattenJsonbPayload` returns an empty string for non-object/non-array inputs
`truncateForIndex` enforces ≤ max bytes on a UTF-8 multibyte string without splitting a code point
`truncateForIndex` is a no-op when input is already under the limit
`buildTsvectorSql` produces SQL containing `process_large_lexemes(`, weight 'A' for title, 'B' for subtitle, 'C' for body
Registry exposes exactly 27 indexers via `allIndexers()`; `getIndexer('client')` returns the client indexer
`upsertSearchDoc` inserts a new row when none exists for (tenant, type, id)
`upsertSearchDoc` updates an existing row, refreshing `search_vector` and `indexed_at`
`deleteSearchDoc` removes the row and is a no-op when none exists
Concurrent `upsertSearchDoc` calls for the same (tenant, type, id) end up with one row (last write wins)
Client indexer loadOne returns a SearchDoc with title=client_name and required_permission='client:read'
Backfilling client indexer for a seeded tenant produces an index row for every client in that tenant
Contact indexer subtitle includes email and phone
User indexer excludes users with `user_type='client'`
Ticket indexer denormalizes client_name into subtitle and exposes ticket_number in metadata.identifier
Ticket-comment indexer sets is_internal_only=true when source `comments.is_internal` is true
Ticket-comment indexer url ends with `#comment-{comment_id}`
Project indexer sets `client_scope_id` to the project's client_id
Project phase/task indexers inherit ACL fields from their parent project
Project-task-comment indexer prefers markdown_content over BlockNote content when both are present
Project-task-comment indexer falls back to `flattenBlockNote(note)` when markdown_content is null
Asset indexer's body contains flattened jsonb attribute string values but never key names like `password`
Asset indexer metadata.identifier equals asset_tag
Invoice indexer denormalizes client_name in subtitle and metadata.identifier equals invoice_number
Invoice-item and annotation indexers inherit invoice client_scope_id and required_permission
Contract indexer with `contracts.status='draft'` sets subtitle to 'Quote'
Contract indexer with `contracts.status='active'` sets subtitle to 'Contract'
Document indexer body length is ≤ 65536 bytes even when source content is multi-MB
Document indexer sets `client_scope_id` from `documents.client_id` when non-null; leaves `is_private` and `visible_to_user_ids` at defaults (false / empty)
KB article indexer pulls content via the FK join to documents
Service catalog indexer includes flattened `attributes` jsonb string values in body
Service-request-submission indexer flattens submitted_payload string fields but excludes secret-like keys
Service-request-definition indexer requires admin permission via required_permission column
Workflow-task indexer populates visible_to_user_ids with all assignee user_ids
Schedule-entry indexer populates visible_to_user_ids with the owner
Time-entry indexer SKIPS rows where notes is NULL or empty string (no index row produced)
Time-entry indexer produces a row when notes is any non-empty string (even a single character)
Board, Category, Tag indexers produce rows with only required_permission='ticket:read'
Creating a client publishes `CLIENT_CREATED` event with tenant + client_id
Updating a client publishes `CLIENT_UPDATED`
Deleting a client publishes `CLIENT_DELETED`
Contact CRUD emits CONTACT_* events
User CRUD and role-change emit corresponding events
Project CRUD and child entity CRUD emit project-family events
Asset CRUD emits ASSET_* events
Invoice CRUD (header, items, annotations) emits invoice-family events
Contract / client-contract CRUD emits contract-family events
Document content-change AND share-list-change both emit DOCUMENT_UPDATED
Service catalog CRUD emits SERVICE_CATALOG_* events
Service-request submission / definition CRUD emits corresponding events
Workflow task CRUD + assignment-change emit corresponding events
Interaction, schedule, time-entry, board/category/tag CRUD emit corresponding events
Subscriber starts subscribed to every `sourceEvents` set returned by registered indexers (union of events)
A `CLIENT_CREATED` event causes an upsert into `app_search_index` with object_type='client'
A `CLIENT_DELETED` event causes the index row to be deleted
With `SEARCH_INDEX_LIVE=false`, events are received but `app_search_index` is not written to
Flipping `SEARCH_INDEX_LIVE=true` without restart picks up live writes (or is documented to require a restart)
Subscriber handles a missing source row (race: row deleted before loadOne resolves) by deleting the index row instead of erroring
TICKET_UPDATED triggers a cascade re-index of all of the ticket's comments (their denormalized parent title updates)
INVOICE_UPDATED triggers cascade re-index of items and annotations
PROJECT_UPDATED triggers a paged async re-index covering all phases/tasks/task-comments
Inserting a `document_associations` row tying a document to a different client triggers re-index of the document row with updated `client_scope_id`
User role change kicks off background re-index of rows where the user appears in `visible_to_user_ids`
`npm run search:backfill -- --tenant=<uuid> --type=client` indexes all clients for that tenant
Backfill processes a 10k-row table in batches of 500 without OOM
Running backfill twice produces identical row content (idempotent)
Backfill without --tenant flag iterates all tenants discovered via tenant catalog
`npm run search:backfill` is wired in root package.json
Reconciliation re-indexes a row whose `source_updated_at` advanced after the last index update
Reconciliation deletes an index row whose source row was deleted directly via SQL (event missed)
Reconciliation re-creates an index row that was manually deleted while source still exists
Reconciliation job is registered with pg-boss at startup with a daily cron schedule
parseQuery rejects queries > 200 chars with a typed error
parseQuery accepts and normalizes whitespace and casing
FTS branch returns matches via `search_vector @@ websearch_to_tsquery(...)`
FTS results ordered by `ts_rank_cd` desc
pg_trgm fallback returns results when FTS finds nothing but title is a similar string (`exhcange` → `Exchange`)
pg_trgm similarity is included in the composite score when present
Query `TIC-1023` pins the matching ticket as result #1 via metadata identifier match
Asset-tag style query (e.g., `LAP-0042`) pins the matching asset
All else equal, newer rows rank higher than older rows by ~time-decay factor
Time decay multiplier never drops below 0.05 (floor)
Cursor encoding round-trip is stable; decoded cursor reproduces page boundary correctly
Pagination is stable across two pages: row appearing on page 1 does not reappear on page 2
ts_headline output contains the configured sentinel tokens around matched terms in the body
Sanitizer rebuild produces output containing ONLY `<mark>` tags around match segments; given source body with literal `<script>` text, output contains escaped `<script>`, no executable HTML
Sanitizer is robust to unpaired sentinels (malformed ts_headline output) — falls back to fully escaped output instead of throwing
Typeahead action SQL does NOT include ts_headline (verified via captured query string or telemetry)
acl_predicate excludes rows whose `required_permission` is not in the user's permission set
User permission set is resolved exactly once per action call (not per-row)
Row with non-empty `visible_to_user_ids` is excluded for users not in the array
Row with empty `visible_to_user_ids` is visible to all users with the required permission
`is_internal_only=true` row hidden from a client-type user
Synthetic row with `is_private=true` and non-empty `visible_to_user_ids` is hidden from users not in the array (validates the column is wired, even though no v1 entity sets it)
`client_scope_id` filters out rows for clients the user has no access to
verifyResultVisibility drops rows that the SQL filter let through but the record-level helper rejects
`search.acl_drift` telemetry counter increments when a discrepancy occurs
verifyResultVisibility is a no-op when SQL filter and record-level checks agree (zero drift)
searchAppAction returns SearchAppResult with grouped counts per object_type
searchAppAction throws when called unauthenticated (via withAuth)
searchAppAction enforces tenant isolation: a row from tenant B is never returned to a user in tenant A across 10k seeded queries
searchAppTypeaheadAction returns ≤5 results, title-only, no snippet field
searchAppTypeaheadAction p50 latency < 100ms against a seeded medium tenant
Zod input rejects empty query, > 200 chars, types not in the union, limit > 100
Result rows include a non-empty `url` for every entity type
SearchPalette renders at the top of the MSP sidebar
Cmd+K (mac) and Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) open the SearchPalette and focus the input
Typeahead shows up to 5 rows; each row is a native `<a>` with non-empty `href`
Cmd/Ctrl+click on a typeahead row opens the result in a new tab without closing the palette
Last typeahead row reads `See all N results` (i18n) and links to `/msp/search?q={query}`
Typeahead does NOT render before query length ≥ 2
GET /msp/search?q=foo renders server-side with results in the initial HTML
Typing in the results-page input updates the URL via router.replace, debounced 200ms
Opening a deep-linked /msp/search?q=foo&type=ticket&cursor=...&sort=recent restores the same state
Filter chips render with count badges that match `groups` from the action response
type=All renders grouped sections with max 10 rows per group
type=ticket renders a flat paginated list of tickets only
Next/prev cursor pagination moves forward and back without duplicating rows
Empty state echoes the query back and suggests removing the type filter
Loading skeleton renders on first input change and disappears when results arrive
Every results-page row is a native `<a href>`; Cmd/Ctrl+click opens in new tab
sort=recent orders results by source_updated_at DESC, ignoring relevance score
Sidebar input exposes ARIA combobox role, aria-expanded reflects open state, aria-activedescendant updates on arrow keys
Down-arrow on the sidebar input moves focus into typeahead; up-arrow at top wraps back to input
Esc closes typeahead and restores focus to the input
Enter on input (no row focused) navigates to /msp/search?q=...
Enter on a focused row navigates to that row's url
Stable id `app-search-input` is present on the sidebar input
Every result row has id matching `app-search-result-row-{type}-{id}` (kebab-case)
Every filter chip on the results page has id matching `app-search-filter-chip-{type}`
`server/public/locales/en/msp/core.json` includes all required `search.*` keys (placeholder, helper, group labels, empty state, error, see-all-results)
Lang-pack validation script reports zero missing keys across all locales for the `search` namespace
Sidebar and results page contain zero hardcoded English strings (verified by grep guard test)
Pseudo locale (`xx`) renders accented characters for every search UI string (no untranslated leaks)
Env reference doc lists `SEARCH_INDEX_LIVE` with default and effect described
Deploy runbook walks through migrate → deploy → backfill → flip env → enable UI
Each search action call increments `search.query.count` and records `search.query.latency_ms`
Queries returning zero results increment `search.query.empty`
Rate limit on typeahead action returns a 429-equivalent error after 30 calls/sec from one user
Rate limit on full search returns a 429-equivalent error after 10 calls/sec from one user
Navigating to `/msp/tickets/{id}#comment-{cid}` scrolls the comment into view and applies a `.search-highlight` class for ~2s
`/msp/invoices/{id}#item-{iid}` and `#annotation-{aid}` scroll and highlight correctly
`/msp/projects/{pid}/tasks/{tid}#comment-{cid}` scrolls and highlights correctly
End-to-end: seed a tenant with clients + tickets; search 'acme' as the technician → ACME Corp is top result (Acceptance #1)
End-to-end: pressing Enter on the sidebar input lands on `/msp/search?q={query}` (Acceptance #2)
End-to-end: Cmd/Ctrl+click on a result opens in a new tab and original tab keeps its state (Acceptance #3)
End-to-end: 'TIC-1023' and 'tic-10' both find the right ticket (Acceptance #4)
End-to-end: searching a phrase that appears only in one ticket comment lands on the ticket page with the comment scrolled+highlighted (Acceptance #5)
End-to-end: an internal user sees an internal-comment hit in results (Acceptance #6)
End-to-end: a non-internal user DOES NOT see the same internal-comment hit (Acceptance #7)
End-to-end: user A searches for project they don't have permission for; project does NOT appear (Acceptance #8)
End-to-end: client-scoped user A searches for a document whose `documents.client_id = X` where A has no access to X; document does NOT appear (Acceptance #9)
End-to-end: 'exhcange' returns 'Exchange' as a top result via pg_trgm (Acceptance #10)
End-to-end: creating a ticket through the UI causes it to appear in search within 5 seconds (Acceptance #11)
End-to-end: deleting a ticket causes it to disappear from search within 5 seconds (Acceptance #12)
End-to-end: fresh DB + backfill across all 27 entity types — a sampled record from each type is findable in search (Acceptance #13)
End-to-end: manually DELETE a single index row then run reconciliation → the row is restored (Acceptance #14)
Load test: 1M random-tenant queries across a 50-tenant seed; zero cross-tenant leaks (Acceptance #15)
Pseudo locale renders all search UI strings (Acceptance #16)
Keyboard-only flow can: open palette, type, navigate, choose, open results page, paginate, filter, exit — without using mouse (Acceptance #17)
Deep-linked URL `/msp/search?q=...&type=...&cursor=...&sort=...` reproduces the same results when opened cold (Acceptance #18)
Cross-shard query: searching across a tenant with 10M index rows uses GIN index (EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows no seq scan)
upsertSearchDoc co-locates writes by tenant under Citus (no inter-shard write fanout for a single doc)
Search query SQL always includes `tenant = ?` predicate (verified by SQL log capture)
Document indexer fixture: 10MB BlockNote JSON with embedded image data URI → indexed body ≤ 64KB and contains zero `data:image` substrings
Service-request submission fixture with a 'password' key in submitted_payload → that value is NOT present in the indexed body
`process_large_lexemes` is invoked in the SQL path that computes search_vector (verified via SQL log capture)
Smoke test runs backfill against a seed tenant and then flips SEARCH_INDEX_LIVE=true and confirms incremental updates land
Stress test: 100 ticket updates/sec for 60s causes < 30s indexing lag p99 (subscriber keeps up)
Identifier query containing both an entity ID and a free-text term ranks the exact-id row #1 and free-text matches after it
Search action result includes only entity types the user has permission to read (types union pre-filtered)
Action rejects malformed cursor with a typed error (no 500)
Renaming a ticket title triggers comment row re-index — comment row subtitle reflects new ticket title
Removing a user from a document share-list triggers the document row's `visible_to_user_ids` to update within 10 minutes
Axe-core accessibility scan on SearchPalette reports zero serious/critical violations
Axe-core scan on /msp/search reports zero serious/critical violations
search.query.latency_ms histogram has p95 < 500ms on a seeded medium tenant
search.acl_drift counter is zero across the entire integration test suite (no SQL/record-level mismatch)
Reconciliation job emits a per-run summary log: rows added, rows re-indexed, rows deleted per (tenant, type)
Client-contract indexer joins `clients` and `contracts`; produces title `{client_name} – {contract_name}` and sets `client_scope_id = client_id`
Interaction indexer on a row with BlockNote JSON in `notes` produces a body containing the flattened text from text leaves (e.g., 'Added Sciton Tribrid Laser') and contains no JSON syntax characters
Workflow-task indexer correctly upserts and reads rows when `workflow_tasks.task_id` is the only PK column; queries always include `tenant` in WHERE clause
Workflow-task indexer parses `assigned_users` JSONB into a uuid[] and writes it into `visible_to_user_ids`
CE build: `eeIndexers` resolves to `[]` and `allIndexers().length === ceIndexers.length` (== 27 at v1)
Adding a synthetic indexer via direct `ceIndexers` array, `allIndexers()` includes it; `getIndexer('synthetic')` returns it

Inserting a synthetic row with `object_type='ee_chat_history'` directly via SQL and querying as a CE build (no indexer registered for that type) → row is NOT returned (filtered by `registeredObjectTypes()`)
Results page filter chips render exactly one chip per type in `registeredObjectTypes()` plus an `All` chip; no hard-coded chip list survives a registry change
When a registered object_type has no i18n key for its label, the chip falls back to a humanized form of the object_type (e.g., `service_request_submission` → `Service request submission`)

Reconciliation: inserting a synthetic `app_search_index` row with `object_type='ee_chat_history'` does NOT cause the CE reconciliation job to error or attempt to load a source row for that type; the row remains untouched
